heading: ReMemory
opening: ReMemory started with a question I couldn't stop thinking about: what happens to my digital life if something happens to me?
summary_1: It's a digital safe with multiple keys. You choose who holds them and how many must come together to open it — three of five, two of two, whatever fits. No single person can access anything alone.
summary_2: Each person gets a bundle with everything they need. Recovery is opening a file in a browser — offline*, no accounts, no servers. If this website disappears, recovery still works.
bg_1: Two things led me to build ReMemory.
bg_2: When I watched a documentary about Clive Wearing, a man who has lived with severe amnesia since 1985, it changed how I thought about my own digital life. Memory is more fragile than we like to believe.
bg_3: I've had several concussions from cycling accidents. Each time, I've been lucky to recover fully — but the reminders add up.
bg_4: ReMemory came out of that: a way to make sure the people I trust can access what matters — without putting all of that weight on any one of them.
bg_changelog: ReMemory keeps growing. Check the changelog to see what's new.
how_subtitle: You choose who to trust and how many must agree. Your files are encrypted, the key is split into pieces, and each person keeps one. When enough of them come together, the files are unlocked.
how_caption: Any three of five can recover the files
how_fallback:
Your File → Encrypt → Split key into 5 pieces → Distribute to friends
↓
Any 3 friends → Combine pieces → Unlock → File recovered
bundles_desc: Each person receives a self-contained bundle — a ZIP file with a recovery tool that works in any browser, offline, with no installation. If this project disappears, recovery still works.
bundles_contacts: Every bundle includes contact details for the other people involved, so they can coordinate without you.
trust_1: Recovery works without this project existing — each bundle is self-contained
trust_2: The code is open source — read it on GitHub
trust_3: A security self-review explains the cryptographic choices
trust_4: Everything runs locally. Your files never leave your device
trust_5: Try the demo bundles before using it with anything important
trust_6: Encryption uses age — a modern, well-regarded tool
trust_7: Read more about what protects your data and what could go wrong
try_step_3: Drag Bob's and Camila's README files onto the page
try_step_4: When enough pieces are added, the files unlock
try_docker_link: See the self-hosting guide for details.
try_note: This is the closest thing to what a real recovery feels like.
whatis_is_1: A tool that runs in your browser, offline*
whatis_is_2: A digital safe with multiple keys, held by people you trust
whatis_is_4: Self-contained — recovery works without this website
whatis_isnt_2: An account system or cloud product
whatis_isnt_3: Something that stores your data anywhere
footer_timelock: * Time-locked archives need a brief internet connection at recovery time.
